reasoning: The average of 11.25 events per 30 days over a 12-month period suggests an underlying rate around 11. Conflict event counts typically follow a right-skewed distribution (such as a Negative Binomial), where the median is lower than the mean. Because the target is strictly 'more' (i.e., >= 12 events), the probability of exceeding the historical mean in any single future 30-day period is generally less than 50% due to this right skew. Assuming an moderately overdispersed distribution with a mean of 11.25, the probability of observing 12 or more events is approximately 0.40 to 0.45. Therefore, a forecast of 0.41 appropriately reflects both the Poisson/Negative Binomial base rate and typical right-skewed event distributions in conflict data.
